title: Moist Brazilian Fish Tagine
description: A floral and juicy Brazilian dish featuring treacle and cooked in a tagine, wrapped in lotus leaves, perfect for pescatarians. This recipe combines the richness of fish with the sweetness of treacle and the freshness of lotus leaves.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together the treacle, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Place a cod fillet in the center of each lotus leaf, leaving a 1-inch border around the fish.
Brush the treacle mixture evenly over both sides of the cod fillets.
Top each cod fillet with a few slices of onion, a sprinkle of minced garlic, and a sprinkle of grated ginger.
Fold the lotus leaves over the fish to form a tight packet, and secure with kitchen twine if needed.
Place the packets in a large tagine or Dutch oven, and add 1 cup of water to the bottom of the pan.
Cover the pan and bake for 20 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Remove the packets from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Serve the fish hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley and a drizzle of the cooking liquid.
